_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to compare active user accounts on a network to appropriate Standard Operating Procedure (SOP), gather active user accounts on a network and compare to authorized user list
reference_document_element_description: Skill in mapping personal data flows across systems, vendors, and jurisdictions to produce records of processing activities (RoPA) in compliance with regulatory requirements.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-S-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-T-015 (DG, cosine: 0.330), SP-T-002 (SP, cosine: 0.314).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.7/10 (raw 0.474). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [ability, accounts, active, appropriate, authorized, compare] vs WIDAI-only terms [across, activities, compliance, data, flows, jurisdictions] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.852 (P=0.849, R=0.855). Balanced P/R — the 28-word DCWF element and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). "Ability to compare active user accounts on a network to appropriate Standard Ope" and "Skill in mapping personal data flows across systems, vendors, and jurisdictions" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.367 < STS 0.474 (gap -0.106). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [ability, accounts, active] and [across, activities, compliance] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.3%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [ability, accounts, active, appropriate, authorized, compare]. WIDAI terms: [across, activities, compliance, data, flows, jurisdictions].
